channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Federal government's extra $4b to SRL falls short of state's request
item.description: The federal government's contribution to Victoria's most expensive project in history has risen to $6 billion but falls short of the state government's request.

media:group.media:description: Construction on the Suburban Rail Loop has already begun and is expected to be complete by 2035.

item.title: Labor absence opens door for One Nation and independent in Farrer
item.description: The Labor Party has opted not to run a candidate in tomorrow's Farrer by-election, which will result in a redistribution of votes as traditional Labor supporters are forced to look elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Ashlee Aldridge and Gaye Pattison

media:group.media:description: The Liberals, Nationals, One Nation and an independent are among those contesting the Farrer by-election tomorrow.

item.title: Players raise fears of French Open boycott
item.description: The world's leading tennis players do not feel respected, Jannik Sinner says, as fears grow of the French Open being boycotted.

media:group.media:description: Jannik Sinner has spoken out amid the stoush over pay.

item.title: More delays to Tasmanian government's greyhound ban legislation
item.description: The Tasmanian government's plan to end greyhound racing in the state is facing further delays as the premier asks a parliamentary committee to inquire into it for a second time, amid a hope of winning more support.

media:group.media:description: Premier Jeremy Rockliff wants the ban but says even if it isn't legislated, TasRacing will stop funding and supporting the sport past 2029, effectively ending it.

item.title: Smashed by 1,000 points in four matches, these Dogs are refusing to lie down
item.description: The Dennington Bulldogs are a 115-year-old club that some fear might not make it to 116. But there's more to the Bulldogs' beltings this season than meets the eye.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Miles

media:group.media:description: Dennington lost its fourth match in a row when it came up against Russell's Creek football club

item.title: Aussie racers debut in Japanese all-female series
item.description: The next chapter for two promising Australian motor racing talents begins this weekend as Japan's Kyojo Cup commences its new season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Joanne Ciconte in the number 10 car during pre-season testing for the 2026 Kyojo Cup at the Fuji Speedway.

item.title: SA teacher who lost long service leave while caring for son says system disadvantages women
item.description: A break in service of just over three months to look after her baby son meant contract teacher Rachel Toyer was ineligible for long-service leave. The department has since clarified its rules, allowing for a 15-month break for parental leave.
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Leckie

media:group.media:description: Rachel Toyer moved to South Australia after deciding to become a solo IVF mum by choice.

item.title: 'Completely unprecedented': Crimes against humanity charges new for Australia
item.description: The ABC can reveal new detail about the alleged treatment of a Yazidi woman who claims she was held captive in the home of ISIS-linked Australians in Syria, including two women who are now facing slave-related charges.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie March and Emily Clark

media:group.media:description: Three women who left the Al Roj camp in Syria arrived at Melbourne Airport on Thursday evening.

item.title: Cold snap grips Queensland as temperatures drop below zero
item.description: A chilly southern air mass has most of southern Queensland shivering through the coldest morning of the year so far.
item.dc:creator: Dan McCray

media:group.media:description: Jailei Moy, 10, helped stack firewood for her family business as orders poured in before of the cold snap.

item.title: After 8 years, decision on $30b gas project off WA coast looms
item.description: Woodside's proposal to open up Australia's largest untapped gas resource, the Browse Basin, could be handed to the environment minister for a final decision as soon as next month.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: Woodside intends to build two floating production storage and offloading facilities for the Browse Project.

item.title: Short-stay levy passes Tasmania's lower house with support of everyone except Labor
item.description: Tasmania is one step closer to introducing a levy on short-stay accommodation, after legislation passed the lower house. But a push from the crossbench to direct the funds to crisis accommodation and homelessness services has failed.
item.dc:creator: Lucy MacDonald

media:group.media:description: A proposal for a 5 per cent levy on short-stay accommodation has passed the Tasmanian lower house.

item.title: Husband of woman allegedly murdered overseas accused of forging her death certificate
item.description: The estranged husband of an Australian woman, who police suspect was murdered overseas, has been charged in Brisbane after allegedly forging her death certificate.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: Rehana Parvin's estranged husband Awlad Hossain has been charged with forging her death certificate.

item.title: Family battling cancer relies on donations to pay for in-home child care
item.description: The government is accused of breaking its promise of cheaper child care, with prices rising for 810 vulnerable families.
item.dc:creator: Conor Duffy and Rhiannon Hobbins

media:group.media:description: Emilia and Alyssa are both undergoing treatment for leukaemia, and need to be kept safe at home to avoid illness.

item.title: Super benefits are being paid out to domestic violence perpetrators
item.description: The federal government is reviewing superannuation laws that could see retirement savings paid to domestic violence perpetrators. But industry experts are worried the draft policy reform could only make matters worse.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: There are concerns DV perpetrators could get benefit from flawed superannuation regulations.

item.title: Russia using nuclear diplomacy to build network of dependents
item.description: Russia is building nuclear power plants across the globe, and it's helping it build a network of dependence.
item.dc:creator: Lachlan Bennett

media:group.media:description: Russia has become the leading exporter of nuclear technology across the globe.

item.title: Woman charged with terror-related offences after arriving in Sydney
item.description: A 32-year-old woman has been charged with terror-related offences, after arriving at Sydney International Airport yesterday evening.

media:group.media:description: Authorities say a 32-year-old woman has been charged with terror-related offences after arriving in Sydney.

item.title: The Farrer result will set off willy-willy of spin. The budget already has
item.description: The run-up to a budget always sees a degree of chaos. But this year it has looked like one of those willy-willies that blow up in outback Australia when the wind stirs a storm of dust.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Grattan

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers will deliver the budget on Tuesday, stirring the usual storm of spin.

item.title: Trusts are a 'breakfast buffet' for reducing tax. Is that about to change?
item.description: Tuesday's federal budget is set to contain changes to the rules for trust funds, which economists say are a popular vehicle for tax planning.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim CHalmers is poised to unveil changes to the taxation of trusts in Tuesday's budget.

item.title: Trump wanted an American pope but not this American pope
item.description: Foreign leaders and lobbyists thought they would write the next chapter in Catholic history. They failed and Leo is proving to be the pope that some of them feared.
item.dc:creator: Andrew West

media:group.media:description: Foreign leaders and lobbyists thought they would write the next chapter in Catholic history. They failed and Leo is proving to be the pope that some of them feared.

item.title: Removing capital gains tax discount will be 'disastrous' for small business
item.description: Some entrepreneurs argue any changes to reduce the capital gains tax discount should carve out the start-up sector. However, tax experts warn this would create too much complexity in an already complicated tax system.
item.dc:creator: Nassim Khadem

media:group.media:description: Sally-Ann Williams says a change to the CGT discount would make it harder for startups to attract talent.

item.title: What the tax reform duo could mean for renters
item.description: With changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax coming in this year's federal budget, we spoke to three experts who explained how this will impact rental prices.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: Changes to CGT and negative gearing have renters worried that prices will increase.

item.title: Australia on cusp of housing downturn as demand softens
item.description: The tight supply conditions that have underpinned the national housing market in recent years are starting to ease, according to property data firm Cotality.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: Cotality property analysts warn a housing downturn is looking more and more likely.

item.title: ISIS-linked women willing to take the 'hit' of arrests for their kids' freedom
item.description: When a group of so-called "ISIS brides" boarded a flight from Doha to Melbourne, they took a leap of faith, unsure of what fate awaited them at the end of their journey. But they said they were willing to face arrest for the freedom of their children.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Rollason

media:group.media:description: Part of the group boarded a connecting flight from Doha to Melbourne.

item.title: ABC weekly news quiz: A stranded ship, an underwhelming space show and a Met movie moment
item.description: Don't bother looking up at the sky, it won't be worth it. Instead, try your luck testing your news knowledge from the past week.

media:group.media:description: Sabrina Carpenter during the Met Gala, an annual fundraising gala held for the benefit of the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute with this year's theme 'Costume Art' in New York City, New York, US, May 4, 2026.

item.title: Urzila Carlson thinks your mum is funny, and you should too
item.description: Urzila Carlson's start in comedy was an accident, but now the stand-up regular is bringing sketch comedy to our TV screens.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Safioleas

media:group.media:description: Urzila Carlson says if you don't find women funny you are a "poor bastard".
